serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate serving of honey chicken is approximately 250 g, representing a typical single-person restaurant or takeaway portion.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, cooked, stir-fried (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods), adapted with honey-glaze sauce composition
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for honey-glazed chicken exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken (stir-fried, meat only) combined with estimated honey-glaze sauce contribution (honey, soy sauce, oyster sauce). Input record values (energy 192 kcal, protein 14 g, fat 10 g, carbohydrate 11.2 g) are consistent with this adapted profile and are retained. Secondary micronutrient values (calcium 12 mg, iron 0.8 mg, cholesterol 44 mg, saturated fat 2.4 g, trans fat 0.08 g, vitamin C 1.2 mg) are also consistent with USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken with sauce. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and other micronutrients not reported in input are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Analogous estimate based on honey (GI ~55) and mixed chicken dish with sauce.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for honey-glazed chicken. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the dominant glycaemic driver being honey (GI ~55 per University of Sydney GI Database) combined with the moderating effect of protein (14 g/100g) and fat (10 g/100g) from chicken and sauce. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 28.0) / 100 = 15.4, where carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 11.2 × 2.5 = 28.0 g. GI 55 is considered low according to the University of Sydney GI Database classification (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 380 mg/100g from soy sauce and oyster sauce, with low potassium and fibre offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 100–150 g and request reduced soy sauce and oyster sauce to lower sodium intake, as a full 250 g serving delivers approximately 950 mg sodium — nearly 40% of the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 7.2 g/100g from honey glaze with low dietary fibre (0.4 g/100g), yielding glycemic load of 15.4 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit honey chicken to a half-plate portion (approximately 125 g) and pair it with high-fibre vegetables to blunt the glycaemic response, as the low-fibre honey-based glaze contributes to moderate-to-rapid glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 380 mg/100g, saturated fat 2.4 g/100g, trans fat 0.08 g/100g, and potential deep-frying preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request pan-fried or baked preparation to avoid additional trans fats from deep-frying, limit portion to 150 g or less to manage sodium and saturated fat intake, and avoid regular consumption given the combined sodium and fat burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Honey Chicken (蜜汁鸡肉; Thai: ไก่น้ำผึ้ง) is a popular Thai-influenced chicken dish served in Singapore food courts, hawker centres, and casual dining restaurants. The dish features bite-sized or sliced chicken pieces — typically boneless thigh or breast meat — that are pan-fried or deep-fried until golden, then coated in a glossy sweet-savoury honey glaze made from honey, soy sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, and ginger. The result is tender, juicy chicken with a caramelised exterior that is simultaneously sweet, salty, and mildly aromatic. Sesame seeds are commonly scattered on top as a finishing garnish. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 192 kcal, 14 g protein, 10 g fat (2.4 g saturated), 11.2 g carbohydrate, and 7.2 g sugar, with sodium at 380 mg. The glycaemic index is estimated at 55 (low) driven primarily by the honey glaze. Clinically, the dish is moderate concern for type 2 diabetes due to the low-fibre high-sugar glaze, high concern for hypertension due to sodium from soy and oyster sauces, and moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to combined sodium and fat load. It is halal-certified in most Singapore outlets. A standard serving is approximately 250 g (1 plate), delivering around 480 kcal and 950 mg sodium.
common_user_queries: How many calories in honey chicken?
Is honey chicken high in sodium?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for honey-glazed chicken exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked chicken (stir-fried) with estimated honey-glaze sauce contribution. Input record values are internally consistent and plausible for this dish type. GI is an analogous estimate based on honey as the primary glycaemic driver. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, mineral panel, B-vitamin panel, and moisture data for this honey-glazed chicken preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.08 g/100g from input record; this is a trace level consistent with small amounts from frying oil and is retained. All core macros (protein_g 14.0, fat_g 10.0, carbohydrate_g 11.2) are non-zero and nutritionally consistent with a cooked chicken dish with honey-based sauce — no zero-macro justification required. The dish is sold under the menu name '505D Honey Chicken 蜜汁鸡肉' on GrabFood Singapore. Cultural classification is Thai-style cuisine prepared and sold in Singapore. Halal dietary tag is preserved from input record. GI estimated at 55 (low) using honey GI from University of Sydney GI Database as primary glycaemic driver, moderated by protein and fat content. GI 55 is considered low according to the University of Sydney GI Database classification (low ≤55, medium 56–69, high ≥70). Ontology match types corrected to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 to comply with allowed values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'].
